title: Telosma celebica
intro: Telosma celebica, also known as the "Yellow Trumpet Vine," is a type of flowering vine native to tropical Asia, characterized by its bright yellow, trumpet-shaped flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting various health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Telosma celebica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Telosma celebica is commonly used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional remedies such as syrup and ointments.
taxonomy: Telosma celebica is classified as a species within the Asclepiadoideae subfamily of the Apocynaceae family, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. The Asclepiadoideae subfamily is further divided into several tribes, with Telosma celebica belonging to the Oxystyles tribe. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flowers and seed pods. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 400 genera and 4,000 species. The classification of Telosma celebica reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within this family.
